data.title: AWS expands deal with Saudi AI firm Humain to deploy Nvidia, Trainium chips
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) Web Services said on Wednesday that it has expanded its deal with Saudi Arabian artificial intelligence firm Humain to deploy Nvidia (NVDA) GPUs, as well as Amazon's own Trainium processors.

As part of the deal, AWS and Humain will deploy up to 150,000 accelerators in a data center known as an “AI Zone” in Riyadh. Some of the 150,000 accelerators includes Nvidia's GB300 GPUs. AWS will also become Humain's preferred AI partner globally, and the two companies will bring more AI compute and services from Saudi Arabia to customers worldwide.

“This marks a pivotal moment in our commitment to our partnership with Humain,” said AWS executive Tanuja Randery in a statement. “By combining Humain's local expertise and investment with AWS AI solutions—including our advanced infrastructure, hardware partnerships with NVIDIA, the transformative AI platform Amazon Bedrock, and AI solutions for business users including Amazon Quick Suite—we're establishing a world-class innovation hub that will serve customers across Saudi Arabia and around the globe.”

“The AI Zone represents the beginning of a multi-gigawatt journey for Humain and AWS. From inception, this infrastructure has been engineered to serve both national priorities and the world’s accelerating demand for AI compute,” said Tareq Amin, CEO of Humain. “What truly sets this partnership apart is the scale of our ambition and the innovation in how we work together. Through a groundbreaking commercial model and a shared commitment to global market expansion, we are creating an ecosystem that will shape the future of how AI ideas can be built, deployed, and scaled for the whole world.”

Amazon shares were fractionally lower in afternoon trading, while Nvidia shares were up around 1.5%.

data.title: La-Z-Boy launched higher on FQ3 results, another dividend hike, future outlook
data.description:
La-Z-Boy (LZB) shares reached a 10-week high on Wednesday as better-than-expected fiscal second quarter results, an upbeat fiscal third quarter outlook, and another hike to its dividend elevated the stock by more than 20% and set LZB to close in the green for the week for the first time in four weeks.

“Our operating performance for the second quarter was solid in the midst of a choppy landscape with sales slightly ahead of the midpoint of our guidance and adjusted operating margin that exceeded our expectations,” La-Z-Boy CEO Melinda Whittington said during the company’s third quarter earnings call.

The Michigan-based furniture manufacturer delivered a top- and bottom-line beat and anticipates revenue growth of 1% to 4% for the current fiscal quarter.

However, the increased dividend – its fifth consecutive year of double-digit increases – underscores the company’s commitment to returning cash to shareholders despite an admittedly challenging economic backdrop.

“Our capital allocation target remains consistent to reinvest 50% of operating cash flow back into the business and return 50% to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends,” CFO Taylor Luebke added on the call.

The results led Sidoti’s Anthony Lebiedzinski to upgrade La-Z-Boy (LZB) to Buy from Neutral on greater confidence in the company’s future.

data.title: RTX, TransDigm, AeroVironment lead BNP Paribas’ bullish new calls on U.S. aerospace & defense
data.description:
BNP Paribas Exane on Wednesday initiated coverage of a dozen U.S. aerospace and defense companies, arguing that investors should be selective as commercial aerospace faces tougher comparisons while defense spending is poised to accelerate in 2026. The firm sees greater upside among suppliers and defense-focused contractors than among large commercial original equipment manufacturers, or OEMs.

Senior analyst Matthew Akers said the sector has enjoyed strong book-to-bill momentum, but some of that strength is unlikely to repeat. In commercial aerospace, Exane prefers parts and subsystem suppliers, rating RTX (RTX), TransDigm Group (TDG) and AeroVironment (AVAV) positively, while beginning coverage of Boeing (BA) and GE Aerospace (GE) with negative views. On the defense side, the team expects a 2026 U.S. budget resolution to unlock demand, with Lockheed Martin (LMT), Northrop Grumman (NOC) and AeroVironment (AVAV) highlighted as top picks.

Below are key calls from the new coverage:

AeroVironment (Outperform)

Exane argues the company sits at the center of several Pentagon priorities, from small unmanned aircraft to counter-drone systems and space-related technologies. Demand for loitering munitions and tactical drones has surged in recent conflicts, and the firm expects AeroVironment’s (AVAV) AxS segment to expand at a mid-teens pace.

The rBlueHalo acquisition broadened its footprint into areas such as laser communications, EW, RF satcom and space electronics. Exane values the stock at 7.4 times full-year 2027 revenue, citing a premium warranted by growth prospects.

TransDigm (Outperform)

Analysts see the aircraft-parts manufacturer positioned to outperform conservative estimates, especially in margins for 2026 and 2027. Exane said concerns that TransDigm’s (TDG) acquisition-driven model is running out of runway are misplaced, noting the company still has ample capacity for deals and capital returns.

The firm also assumes a $100 special dividend next year and says a bullish scenario involving more M&A could boost returns to roughly 65%.

RTX (Outperform)

The bank expects stronger-than-projected output at RTX (RTX) unit Collins Aerospace as supply-chain bottlenecks ease, improving prospects for original-equipment sales.

Pratt & Whitney is also expected to gain momentum as the geared turbofan (GTF) fleet matures and legacy V2500 engines remain in service longer than anticipated. Meanwhile, Exane sees room for Raytheon’s defense margins to rise as key programs move into production.

GE Aerospace (Underperform)

Exane takes a cautious stance on GE Aerospace (GE), arguing that the tailwinds from aftermarket mix and cost improvements are fading. The firm expects slower learning-curve gains on LEAP engines and rising losses on the GE9X program as Boeing (BA) ramps the 777X.

The analysts’ model points to weaker growth in out-of-warranty aftermarket revenue later this decade, with fewer LEAP engines aging into peak maintenance years.

L3Harris (Neutral)

Shares of L3Harris (LHX) have climbed significantly in recent quarters, and Exane sees limited room for multiple expansion. While the company is positioned to benefit from missile-defense programs and its Aerojet Rocketdyne unit, the valuation already reflects substantial improvement.

Boeing (Underperform)

The research team acknowledges operational improvements at Boeing (BA) under Chief Executive Kelly Ortberg, but says Wall Street’s expectations for aircraft production and cash flow are too optimistic.

Exane argues Boeing’s (BA) book-to-bill ratio may not hold up as traffic growth moderates and as past, tariff-driven orders roll off. The firm also expects the company’s debt load and upcoming maturities to absorb most free cash flow through the decade.

Lockheed Martin (Outperform)

Lockheed’s (LMT) missile programs and international demand are seen as key growth drivers, particularly under the U.S. “Golden Dome” missile-defense initiative.

Akers also expects space-segment profitability to improve as United Launch Alliance’s Vulcan rocket increases launch cadence, including commitments tied to Amazon’s (AMZN) Kuiper constellation. Concerns about the F-35 program and recent classified charges, he writes, appear fully priced into the stock.

Kratos Defense (Neutral)

Exane recognizes Kratos’ (KTOS) broad exposure to defense-tech categories, from drones to hypersonics, but says the stock has already run significantly and looks expensive relative to peers. Potential upcoming contract wins could add to estimates, but the firm remains cautious about valuation.

General Dynamics (Outperform)

The bank forecasts improving performance for General Dynamics (GD) across Gulfstream business jets, submarine and shipbuilding programs, and its Mission Systems technology arm. Gulfstream’s newer models should lift margins as production ramps, while U.S. efforts to fix shipbuilding bottlenecks are expected to benefit the Marine segment.

Northrop Grumman (Outperform)

Sentiment toward Northrop Grumman (NOC) has been weighed down by this year’s cost charge on the B-21 bomber, but Exane believes the program is moving closer to a more profitable phase.

The analysts expect faster outlay growth on multiple Northrop programs, from missile defense to classified space, and see room for margins to rise as development-heavy work transitions to procurement.

Howmet Aerospace (Outperform)

Howmet’s (HWM) premium valuation is justified, according to the firm, as the company continues to outperform on pricing, share gains and spares demand. Exane sees stronger-than-expected commercial build rates and incremental help from non-aero segments such as industrial gas turbines and commercial wheels.

Heico (Neutral)

The aftermarket-parts supplier remains the leading force in the PMA market, and Exane expects Heico (HEI) to widen that lead. However, the analysts note that big acquisitions may slow until the company reduces leverage, and the stock’s steep valuation drives a more cautious stance.

data.title: Exact Sciences jumps on report Abbott nears takeover
data.description:
Exact Sciences (EXAS) soared 28% on a report that Abbott Laboratories (ABT) is nearing a possible takeover of the medical-testing company. 

Abbott (ABT) is discussing the terms of a deal with Exact Sciences (EXAS), according to a Bloomberg report on Wednesday, which cited people familiar with the matter. Talks are ongoing and could be delayed, or a deal could not come to fruition.

Exact Sciences has a market cap of $13.2 billion.

Abbott and Exact Sciences didn’t immediately respond to Bloomberg requests for comment.

data.title: Powell Industries targets $12.4M Jacintoport expansion as backlog hits $1.4B, eyes continued order momentum
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Powell Industries (POWL) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Brett Cope, Chairman of the Board, President & CEO, highlighted a record year for Powell with "gross profit dollar growth of 16%, revenue growth of 8% and the generation of $61 million in operating cash flow" compared to the prior year's fourth quarter. Cope noted that the company's efforts to diversify have paid off, with nonindustrial markets such as Electric Utility and Commercial and Other Industrial now accounting for 41% of annual revenue and 48% of total backlog. He also reported, "We booked $271 million of new orders in the quarter, which was roughly 1% higher than the prior year."
  • Cope announced the next phase of a $12.4 million investment in the Jacintoport facility, targeting incremental capacity, primarily for anticipated LNG project development. He stated, "We expect this phase of the Jacintoport expansion to be completed by the second half of fiscal 2026."
  • The acquisition of Remsdaq closed during the fiscal fourth quarter, with Cope expressing excitement about scaling the electrical automation strategy and introducing Remsdaq's products to North America.
  • Michael Metcalf, Executive VP, CFO, Secretary & Principal Accounting Officer, stated, "Revenues for the fourth fiscal quarter of 2025 increased by 8% to $298 million compared to the same quarter in fiscal 2024 of $275 million, and was also higher sequentially by $12 million, driven predominantly on the strength across our electric utility sector."

Outlook

  • Cope indicated that "the Commercial environment for each of our end markets remains positive as we are optimistic that the momentum we built throughout our fiscal 2025 will continue into the new year."
  • He highlighted strong fundamentals for Oil and Gas, especially LNG, and noted, "Opportunities are growing in both size and volume as well as product applications as we expand our presence in this strategic market."
  • Metcalf added, "We are confident that the strong commercial momentum we experienced across our key end markets in fiscal 2025 will carry into fiscal 2026."

Financial Results

  • Metcalf reported, "In the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025, we reported net income of $51.4 million, generating $4.22 per diluted share compared to net income of $46 million or $3.77 per diluted share in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024."
  • The company delivered $94 million of gross profit in the fourth quarter, a 16% increase from the prior year, and gross profit as a percentage of revenues was 31.4%.
  • Domestic revenues reached $239 million, and international revenues increased by 38% to $68 million.
  • Operating cash flow for the quarter was $61 million. For the full fiscal year, operating cash flow reached $168 million compared to $109 million in the previous year.
  • Capital spending for property, plant, and equipment totaled $13 million for the year, with $1.8 million in the fourth quarter.
  • The company ended the year with $476 million in cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments and holds no debt.

Q&A

  • John Franzreb, Sidoti & Company: Asked about changes in the competitive landscape and pricing. Cope responded, "It's a tale of different scenarios regionally by different sectors right now."
  • Franzreb inquired about seasonality and first quarter expectations. Metcalf answered, "We do anticipate that sequentially, as we exit fourth quarter and report our first quarter, it seasonally is softer due to what I just mentioned. That said, as we look forward on a total year basis, we still are very optimistic about next year."
  • Franzreb asked about SG&A onetime expenses. Metcalf detailed, "Roughly $3 million of that was due to compensation -- variable compensation items and $2 million -- a little less than $2 million was acquisition-related, legal, valuation services."
  • Alfred Moore, ROTH Capital Partners, LLC, asked about C&I sector trends and data center opportunities. Cope replied, "The opportunities are clearly growing, both for things that we have noted on the earlier calls that we're aspiring to bring to market to get inside the 4 walls of the data center."
  • Moore also asked about utility sector growth sustainability. Cope stated, "We think the demand profile looks good. That includes both where we have been fighting our way into the distribution side of the substation."
  • Moore followed up on data center DC architecture. Cope explained Powell's DC switchgear capabilities and ongoing R&D for rectifier solutions tailored to data centers.
  • Moore questioned margin sustainability. Metcalf replied, "We do anticipate a continuation of solid project execution through next year. And considering this margins in the upper 20s for the total year of fiscal 2026 are realistic."
  • Jon Braatz, Kansas City Capital, asked about LNG project timelines. Cope responded, "It has taken a little more time, to your point, Jon, to spin back up. I think ... I'm not overly worried about it. I still feel really good about the fundamentals on many of the projects."
  • Braatz inquired about C&I and Traction sector drivers. Cope said, "It is largely driven by data centers, and it is growing for us for sure," and noted a timing convergence for new traction projects.
  • Franzreb asked about project closeouts. Metcalf stated, "Closeouts, I would say, in 2025 were a little bit heavier than in prior periods ... 1.7% to be exact."
  • Franzreb requested R&D and CapEx insight. Cope said, "You'll continue to see spending at this level for the next couple of years... I think in '26, I expect to see some products hit the market." Metcalf projected CapEx for 2026 as "the $12.4 million for the Jacintoport expansion ... on top of maintenance and productivity projects ... $5 million to $7 million range."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed a slightly positive tone, frequently congratulating management and inquiring about growth drivers but probing on the sustainability of margins, sector trends, and project closeouts.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone during prepared remarks and Q&A, repeatedly emphasizing strong market momentum and opportunities, while providing detailed responses to questions about sector outlook and operational strategy.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the sentiment remained constructive, with management demonstrating increased confidence about continued momentum and operational execution.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company maintained record performance, with gross profit and revenue growth continuing from Q3 to Q4. The backlog remained strong at $1.4 billion, but the book-to-bill ratio fell from 1.3x in Q3 to 0.9x in Q4, reflecting fewer mega project wins in the latest quarter.
  • Management focus shifted toward expansion initiatives, including a new phase of investment in the Jacintoport facility and the completion of the Remsdaq acquisition. The tone regarding the Electric Utility and data center markets became even more bullish, with increased emphasis on automation and product development.
  • Analysts in both quarters focused on order flow, margin sustainability, and end-market trends, but Q4 included more questions on CapEx, R&D, and the commercialization timeline for new products.
  • Management's tone in Q4 was more specific about strategic investments and their expected impact, while reiterating optimism about market opportunities.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited emerging divergence across key end markets, describing a "global economic environment that is operating at very different speeds, driven by country, region and sector imbalances."
  • Some softness was noted in traditional oil and gas and petrochemical markets, particularly refineries and polyethylene/polypropylene facilities.
  • SG&A expenses increased due to variable compensation and acquisition costs, with Metcalf clarifying that "about $3 million" was compensation-related and "a little less than $2 million was acquisition-related."
  • The company continues to monitor project timing and seasonality, with first quarter softness expected due to holidays.

Final Takeaway

Powell Industries concluded fiscal 2025 with record financial results and a strong backlog, underpinned by strategic investments in capacity expansion and the acquisition of Remsdaq. Management emphasized continued momentum in electric utility, data center, and LNG markets, while noting a balanced backlog and robust pipeline for fiscal 2026. The company remains focused on disciplined execution, margin sustainability, and leveraging its financial strength to support organic and inorganic growth opportunities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Land & Buildings exits VICI Properties, American Tower in Q3
data.description:
Land & Buildings Investment Management exited VICI Properties (VICI) and American Tower (AMT) during the third quarter, according to its latest 13F filing.

Land & Buildings is known as an investment firm specializing in publicly traded real estate and related securities, with an activist approach that often involves pushing for strategic changes within its portfolio companies.

In Q3, the activist investor took a $3.72M stake in AvalonBay Communities (AVB), $35.30M stake in Centerspace (CSR), $12.52M stake in Crown Castle (CCI), $7.77M stake in Empire State Realty Trust (ESRT), $20.60M stake in Gaming and Leisure Properties (GLPI), $24.04M stake in Prologis (PLD), $9.57M stake in Ryman Hospitality Properties (RHP), and a $3.57M stake in UDR (UDR).

Another notable addition was a $36.02M stake in Six Flags Entertainment (FUN).

Also, the investment firm exited Apartment Investment and Management (AIV), Equity Residential (EQR), Kite Realty Group (KRG), Elme Communities (ELME), and SmartRent (SMRT) in Q3.

data.title: J&J, Halozyme win FDA traditional nod for Darzalex Faspro in AL amyloidosis
data.description:
The U.S. FDA on Thursday granted traditional approval for Darzalex Faspro, an injectable version of the multiple myeloma therapy daratumumab, developed by J&J (JNJ) and Halozyme Therapeutics (HALO), as a front-line option for light chain ((AL)) amyloidosis, a rare blood cell disorder.

In 2021, the agency granted accelerated approval for Darzalex Faspro as part of a combination regimen for adults with newly diagnosed AL amyloidosis, marking the first and only FDA-approved treatment for this patient population.

The latest decision, based on J&J’s (JNJ ANDROMEDA Phase 3 trial, converts the accelerated approval to a traditional approval, the FDA said in a statement.

The regulator first approved Darzalex Faspro in 2020 and expanded its label to ten indications in multiple myeloma later, most recently for the smoldering asymptomatic form of the disease early this month.

data.title: Novartis to establish a manufacturing center in North Carolina
data.description:
Novartis (NVS) said it will grow its operations in North Carolina by establishing a new manufacturing hub that will handle the entire production process.

This initiative is part of a larger $23B investment by Novartis in infrastructure across the United States over the next five years.

The goal of this expansion is to boost manufacturing capacity, allowing all of the company's essential medicines to be produced entirely within the US.

The new manufacturing hub is expected to open between 2027 and 2028 and will cover over 700,000 square feet, combining existing facilities with new ones.

This project is expected to generate 700 direct jobs at Novartis and create more than 3,000 additional jobs in the supply chain by the end of 2030.

data.title: Perplexity and PayPal to launch AI-powered shopping assistant
data.description:
Just in time for holiday shopping, Perplexity is partnering with PayPal (PYPL) to provide a free agentic shopping tool that allows users to search for and purchase items from over 5,000 merchants.

The new app will be available for free download next week, according to CNBC.

By employing agentic AI, the new Perplexity app will scrape through browsing history to deliver more personalized search results and enable shoppers to purchase directly from a retailer through the Perplexity search engine.

PayPal (PYPL) merchants will serve as merchants of record to facilitate purchases, customer service, and returns. PayPal (PYPL) will also provide its buyer protection service to items purchased through the Perplexity app.  

PayPal (PYPL) has a similar tool with OpenAI’s ChatGPT called Instant Checkout, integrating PayPal’s (PYPL) wallet and payment processing services with OpenAI’s e-commerce capabilities. While OpenAI receives a fee for these transactions, Perplexity has not disclosed if it will be compensated for directing traffic to retailers.

data.title: Coco Robotics adds Shake Shack to its autonomous delivery robot program at two Chicago locations
data.description:
Coco Robotics announced that it is collaborating with Uber Eats (UBER) and Shake Shack (SHAK) on autonomous deliveries for two locations in the Chicago region.

The partnership will see Coco's robots integrate directly into Shake Shack's (SHAK) existing Uber Eats ordering system, eliminating manual entry or extra steps for in-store teams. Orders move directly from the kitchen to the curb, where a Coco robot completes the final leg of the journey, delivering it directly to the customer. Coco's robots operate on sidewalks and in high-traffic urban areas, safely navigating to customers' designated pickup locations.

By partnering with Coco, Shake Shack (SHAK) said it will be able to fulfill more orders, especially during peak hours, improving delivery timing and ensuring a consistent, seamless experience for guests. The partnership is seen as adding a convenient option to bring the Shack experience to customers at appropriate locations, especially during the colder months. "The robots are intuitive, easy to work with, and bring a little extra joy to delivery," stated Shake Shack Chief Growth Officer Steph So.

Coco Robotics aims to deploy over 10,000 robots by 2026, targeting expansion in the U.S. and Europe. The company is developing higher-capacity robots with multi-temperature compartments to serve groceries, pharmaceuticals, and small parcel e-commerce beyond hot food delivery. Its growth strategy includes deepening partnerships with enterprise retailers and quick-service restaurant chains, as it leverages an AI-as-a-service model that licenses its technology stack to other robotics manufacturers. DoorDash (DASH) is one of the company's significant partners.

Notably, Coco Robotics has raised over $120 million in total funding, including $80 million in its most recent strategic round earlier this year. High-profile investors include OpenAI's (OPENAI) Sam Altman, Max Altman, Pelion Venture Partners, Offline Ventures, Outlander VC, DeepWater Asset Management, and former Uber (UBER) executive Ryan Graves.
